products.name: Go-To-Market Strategy Consulting
recommendation: EQIQ's Go-To-Market Strategy Consulting is a pure professional services offering with no software product to provision, configure, or score in any Skillable environment. The API Automation Gate fails at Step 1 — there is no software platform, no per-learner provisioning path, no accounts to create, and no state to validate. Hands-on labs require a software product; consulting methodology does not qualify. No investment of solution engineering time is warranted.

The research pages provided for this scoring exercise returned content from three unrelated companies — IQ-EQ (fund administration), Epiq (procurement software), and The Emotional Intelligence Training Company (EQ-i 2.0 certification). None of these are EQIQ the GTM consulting firm. If the intent was to score one of these other companies, the product should be resubmitted with correct research. IQ-EQ (fund services) and Epiq (source-to-pay software) would both warrant separate, proper evaluations.

If EQIQ has developed or is developing a proprietary software platform (e.g., a GTM assessment tool, a sales enablement SaaS, or a positioning framework application), that product should be scored separately. The consulting methodology itself is not labable, but a software tool that operationalizes the methodology could be. Confirm with the prospect whether any software product exists before closing the file entirely.

dimensions.summary: No software product exists to provision. EQIQ's Go-To-Market Strategy Consulting is a pure professional services offering — human advisory, workshops, and strategic frameworks. evidence.claim: **No Software Product | Blocker:** EQIQ's Go-To-Market Strategy Consulting is a pure professional services offering with no software component — no installable application, no SaaS platform, no container image, and no cloud service exists to deploy in any Skillable fabric. The 'product' is human advisory and strategic frameworks delivered by consultants.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Service delivery format — Find a Managing Partner or Practice Lead in the Go-to-Market Strategy practice. When you deliver sales enablement engagements to your tech-company clients, do any of those engagements include hands-on exercises where reps interact with live software (e.g., CRM workflows, demo environments, competitive battle-card tools), or is the delivery entirely slide-based coaching and advisory?

Client lab demand signal — Find a Director of Enablement or Senior Consultant who runs client-facing workshops. Have any of your technology-company clients asked you to provide interactive, sandbox-style practice environments (e.g., simulated sales calls, live product demo sandboxes, or guided tool walkthroughs) as part of the enablement programs you build for them?

Productization roadmap — Find the CEO or Head of Product/Offerings. Is EQIQ exploring packaging any of its enablement methodologies as a scalable digital product (self-paced courses, certification programs, or interactive playbooks) rather than purely consultant-delivered engagements?

products.name: Positioning & Messaging Consulting
lab_concepts: Build a Positioning Canvas for a Fictional SaaS Product
recommendation: EQIQ's Positioning & Messaging Consulting is a human-delivered professional service, not a software product. There is no installable application, no cloud environment, no API surface, and no learner-facing digital platform — the fundamental requirement for any Skillable lab path. No provisioning method exists, and the 'product' produces human-readable consulting deliverables (positioning canvases, messaging frameworks) that cannot be validated programmatically. This is not a gap that can be engineered around; it is a category mismatch.

The research returned pages for three unrelated companies — IQ-EQ (financial services fund administration), Epiq (source-to-pay procurement software), and EITC (emotional intelligence certification) — none of which are EQIQ the GTM consulting firm. EQIQ appears to have minimal web presence, which itself is a signal of company scale. Before any further evaluation, confirm the correct company URL and verify that a software product exists before re-scoring.

No meaningful automated scoring path exists. The only viable assessment method is MCQ-based recall of positioning frameworks (e.g., April Dunford's Obviously Awesome methodology, Geoffrey Moore's Crossing the Chasm positioning statement template). AI Vision could theoretically evaluate a completed positioning canvas screenshot, but the instructional value of doing so is negligible compared to the platform overhead cost.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Deliverable format — Find the engagement lead or Principal Consultant who scopes client projects. When you deliver positioning & messaging frameworks to your tech-company clients, is the final output a static document (slide deck, PDF), or do clients ever need to interact with the framework in a live tool—e.g., filling in their own value props against a template in real time?

Repeatability of methodology — Find the Head of Consulting Operations or Practice Lead responsible for scaling delivery. Do you have a standardized, step-by-step workshop methodology (with defined inputs, exercises, and outputs at each stage) that you run consistently across engagements, or is each engagement custom-designed from scratch?

Client enablement gap — Find the VP of Training or Director of Enablement (if one exists) or whoever handles post-engagement adoption. After you hand off positioning & messaging work, do your clients' sales teams struggle to apply the new messaging consistently—and if so, have you explored interactive practice environments (role-play simulations, scenario-based exercises) as a follow-on offering?

evidence.claim: **No Applicable Lab Types | Risk:** None of Skillable's lab format types (Break/Fix, Incident Response, Architecture Challenge, Cyber Range, etc.) apply to a leadership consulting methodology. Role-play and scenario-based learning exist in the L&D world but are outside Skillable's hands-on technical lab model — inferred from service category.

dimensions.name: Market Demand
dimensions.summary: Management consulting and leadership development is a large market, but demand for hands-on technical labs in this category is negligible. No certification program, no competitor labs, no ATP network, and no install base signal relevant to Skillable's lab delivery model.

evidence.claim: **Wrong Category for Labs | Risk:** Leadership development and management consulting is a large market, but demand for hands-on technical labs in this category is negligible. No certification program, no competitor labs, and no install base signal relevant to Skillable's lab delivery model — inferred from category norms.

dimensions.name: Training Commitment
dimensions.summary: EQIQ delivers consulting engagements that include enablement components (sales enablement, positioning, team transformation), but there is no formal training organization, no published course catalog, no certification program, and no evidence of a dedicated learning function. Training is incidental to the consulting delivery model.

evidence.claim: **Consulting Delivery Model | Risk:** EQIQ delivers enablement as part of consulting engagements (sales enablement, team transformation), but there is no formal training organization, published course catalog, or certification program. Training is incidental to consulting delivery, not a standalone business line — eqiq.com product description.

dimensions.name: Organizational Dna
dimensions.summary: As a consulting firm, EQIQ works with external clients and partners by definition. However, the firm appears small (boutique go-to-market consultancy), with no evidence of a formal partner ecosystem, marketplace presence, or platform integrations. Ease of engagement is likely high given small size, but there is no strategic alignment with Skillable's platform model.

evidence.claim: **Boutique Consultancy | Context:** EQIQ appears to be a small boutique go-to-market consulting firm serving technology companies. Small size suggests ease of engagement, but also limited budget, no platform procurement infrastructure, and no strategic alignment with Skillable's lab platform model — eqiq.com company description.

dimensions.name: Delivery Capacity
dimensions.summary: No LMS platform, no ATP network, no lab platform, and no evidence of scaled training delivery infrastructure. EQIQ delivers consulting engagements directly to technology company clients — not through a training distribution channel that could consume Skillable labs at scale.

evidence.claim: **No Delivery Infrastructure | Risk:** No LMS platform, no ATP network, no lab platform, and no evidence of scaled training delivery infrastructure. EQIQ delivers consulting engagements directly — not through a training distribution channel that could consume Skillable labs at scale — eqiq.com company description.

dimensions.name: Build Capacity
dimensions.summary: No content development team, no instructional designers, no lab authors identified. EQIQ's staff are consultants and advisors, not technical content builders. Any lab build would require full Skillable Professional Services engagement — but there is no viable lab to build.

evidence.claim: **No Content Dev Team | Risk:** No instructional designers, lab authors, or technical content builders identified. EQIQ staff are consultants and advisors. Any lab build would require full external resourcing — but there is no viable lab to build given the absence of a software product — eqiq.com company description.

key_technical_questions.heading: Key Technical Questions
bullets: Deliverable format — Find the Director of Enablement or whoever designs the Team Leadership & Transformation program. What is the actual deliverable today — is it live facilitated workshops, a self-paced digital curriculum, or a blended model, and is any portion delivered through a software platform participants log into?

Hands-on component viability — Find the person who owns program design (likely a Principal Consultant or Practice Lead). Are there any interactive exercises, simulations, or tool-based activities in the leadership curriculum that participants complete individually on a computer, or is the entire experience discussion-and-coaching based?

Scale & repeatability — Find whoever manages client delivery operations. How many unique cohorts per year run through Team Leadership & Transformation, and is there demand from your clients to offer a self-service or asynchronous version of any module that currently requires a live facilitator?

account_intelligence.heading: Account Intelligence
bullets: EQIQ specializes in team leadership transformation and sales enablement for tech companies — they're likely running internal training programs and may use labs for client deliverables or internal capability building
As a professional services firm, they probably lack dedicated hands-on lab infrastructure; worth exploring if they're building simulation-based training for clients and need a platform to scale it
